title: The One Where Old Yeller Dies

jokes.text: Why are you guys upset? It's Old Yeller. It's a happy movie.
jokes.setup: Everyone is visibly upset watching Old Yeller, a famously sad movie
jokes.punchline: Phoebe genuinely thinks it's happy because she's never seen the tragic ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her innocent obliviousness creates dramatic irony. Well-structured with clear setup and character-specific punchline.

jokes.text: That's when my mother would shut off the TV and say, 'The end.'
jokes.setup: Explanation of why Phoebe thinks Old Yeller is happy
jokes.punchline: Her mother censored all sad endings to protect her
jokes.explanation: Clever backstory that explains Phoebe's naivety while adding pathos. Character-specific and well-crafted.

jokes.text: He doesn't have rabies. He has babies.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Old Yeller having rabies
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent mishearing/misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay but effective for character. Economical and fits Phoebe's naive worldview.

jokes.text: Oh, no. No, no, Travis, put down the gun. No, no, he's your buddy. He's your Yeller. No! The end! The end!
jokes.setup: Phoebe watches the actual ending of Old Yeller for the first time
jokes.punchline: Her panicked reaction as she realizes what's happening, desperately trying to stop it
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the setup. Phoebe's innocence colliding with harsh reality creates genuine cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Okay, what kind of a sick doggy snuff film is this?
jokes.setup: Phoebe has just witnessed Old Yeller being shot
jokes.punchline: She frames a classic family film as exploitation cinema
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever reframing. 'Doggy snuff film' is unexpected and quotable while capturing Phoebe's shock.

jokes.text: Actually, nothing but rubber gloves.
jokes.setup: Monica offers leftovers and someone asks what she's wearing
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of being naked except for rubber gloves while cooking
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag setup. Not groundbreaking but efficiently constructed and appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: One of these times, you'll really be naked and we won't come over.
jokes.setup: Monica's joke about being naked
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the boy-who-cried-wolf scenario
jokes.explanation: Logical follow-up to Monica's gag. Competent but predictable response.

jokes.text: That teach me a lesson.
jokes.setup: Chandler's threat about not coming over
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic, dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Weak closer to the exchange. Too predictable and doesn't add much comedic value.

jokes.text: How do you find clothes that fit?
jokes.setup: Joey lists chicken parts: 'I got a leg, three breasts and a wing'
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats it as Joey describing his own anatomy
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect timing. Great economy - maximum funny per word. Classic Chandler wit.

jokes.text: All right, yes, I see other women in the shower at the gym. And no, I don't look.
jokes.setup: Someone says they have a question for Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica assumes it's about her sexuality and preemptively answers
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals Monica's defensiveness. Character-appropriate and well-timed.

jokes.text: Did you see that play? You want more beer? Is that Spike Lee?
jokes.setup: Description of Dan as someone 'with the poking'
jokes.punchline: Joey demonstrates the annoying poking behavior
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that works through demonstration. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: That's Eric. Glad to be of help. Matches.
jokes.setup: Richard gives thoughtful advice about choosing the Knicks fan
jokes.punchline: He immediately contradicts himself by asking for matches, showing he wasn't really listening
jokes.explanation: Subtle character comedy showing Richard's detachment. Well-crafted understatement.

jokes.text: Plus, he's old
jokes.setup: Joey starts to say Richard is old
jokes.punchline: The awkward pause as he realizes Richard is right there
jokes.explanation: Basic awkward moment comedy. Works but is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: er than some people. But younger than some buildings.
jokes.setup: Joey's awkward 'he's old' comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's attempt to save the situation with absurd comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler save with absurdist logic. Original comparison that's quotable and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: How do we say yes and make it seem like it's not just to ride in the cool car?
jokes.setup: Monica suggests they invite Richard and mentions his Jaguar
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately focuses on the car while trying to seem like he doesn't
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about their shallow motivations. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: We'll bring him, but only if he takes the Jaguar.
jokes.setup: The challenge of not seeming interested in the car
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately fails by making it about the car
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his lack of subtlety. Predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You almost had it.
jokes.setup: Joey's complete failure at subtlety
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective closer. Good economy but lacks originality.

jokes.text: We know. He already did it last week. You can watch our tape if you want.
jokes.setup: Ross excitedly announces Ben pulled himself up for the first time
jokes.punchline: Carol casually deflates his excitement by revealing it already happened
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing the pain of divorced parenting. Well-structured with good timing.

jokes.text: What else? Has he spoken? Is he driving? Does he have a favorite liqueur?
jokes.setup: Ross realizes he's missed multiple milestones
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity from speaking to driving to drinking alcohol
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure building to absurd climax. Perfect economy and character frustration.

jokes.text: He can't quite say 'mama' yet, but once he said 'Yemen.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ben's speech development
jokes.punchline: The random, inexplicable word choice of a Middle Eastern country
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random and unexpected. 'Yemen' is such a specific, absurd choice that it becomes memorable.

jokes.text: Did you just see that? Did you see? He just waved! He's never waved before!
jokes.setup: Ross is desperate to witness a first
jokes.punchline: He thinks he's seeing a milestone but it's probably not new
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's desperation, but predictable callback to established pattern.

jokes.text: Yes, he has. Very good.
jokes.setup: Ross's excitement about Ben waving
jokes.punchline: Carol's condescending dismissal like talking to a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect delivery of condescension. The 'Very good' is particularly cutting.

jokes.text: All you need now is The Killing Fields and guacamole, and you got a party!
jokes.setup: Phoebe lists sad movies she's planning to watch
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically suggests adding another depressing movie and party food
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between tragic cinema and festive snacks. Classic Chandler sarcasm with good cultural reference.

jokes.text: You know, before she killed herself.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains her grandmother's philosophy about shielding from sadness
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation of her mother's suicide
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Phoebe moment. The casual delivery of devastating information is character-perfect and shocking.

jokes.text: After we stole his lunch money and gave him a wedgie.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if they ditched Richard
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically describes treating an older man like a school bully victim
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist image of grown men bullying an older man. Classic Chandler deflection.

jokes.text: He let us drive his Jaguar. Joey for 12 blocks. Me for 15.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains why Richard is cool
jokes.punchline: The competitive counting of who got to drive longer
jokes.explanation: Shows their childish competitiveness. Simple but character-appropriate humor.

jokes.text: He must like you best.
jokes.setup: Chandler drove 3 blocks more than Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's sincere conclusion from minimal evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - drawing big conclusions from tiny differences. Character-specific.

jokes.text: Hey, Chandler, thanks for showing us to our seats. You're welcome. Hey, Joey, thanks for parking the car. No problem.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Richard's generous tipping
jokes.punchline: They demonstrate by tipping each other for basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy bit showing their newfound appreciation for tipping culture.

jokes.text: He kept my dollar.
jokes.setup: The guys' elaborate tipping demonstration
jokes.punchline: Richard's confused observation about the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective closer to the bit. Richard's confusion is endearing.

jokes.text: A woman I went to college with is the first female blacksmith there. They're a little behind the times in colonial Williamsburg.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions visiting a female blacksmith in Colonial Williamsburg
jokes.punchline: The understatement about historical reenactment being behind the times
jokes.explanation: Dry observation about historical accuracy vs. progress. Smart humor that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Joey, do you know we can see you from here?
jokes.setup: Joey is apparently trying to light a cigar outside
jokes.punchline: He's visible to everyone while thinking he's being discrete
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Joey's lack of subtlety. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You may wanna light it and lose the spatula.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to look cool with a cigar
jokes.punchline: He's still holding a spatula, ruining the effect
jokes.explanation: Great visual contradiction - trying to look sophisticated while holding kitchen equipment.

jokes.text: Not like him, per se. Just not unlike him.
jokes.setup: Accusation that Joey is trying to be like Richard
jokes.punchline: Joey's convoluted logic to deny while admitting it
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey circular logic. The wordplay reveals his confused thinking process.

jokes.text: We flipped for it. I got the cigar, he got the mustache. If we both grew them, we'd look like dorks.
jokes.setup: Question about why Chandler didn't grow a mustache too
jokes.punchline: Their systematic approach to copying Richard, with the ironic 'dork' comment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic with perfect irony - they're already dorks for copying him systematically.

jokes.text: You really sidestepped that land mine.
jokes.setup: Chandler's explanation about not looking like dorks
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic assessment of his 'success'
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response with good military metaphor. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: You hear that? We're the guys!
jokes.setup: Monica mentions Richard going out with 'the guys'
jokes.punchline: Joey's excited realization of their status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment of excitement over simple acceptance. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: With that mustache, he reminds me of Aunt Sylvia. Thank you!
jokes.setup: Monica comments on Joey's mustache
jokes.punchline: Joey takes the comparison to an aunt as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding - taking an insult as praise. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: A while ago, I got a 'seh' out of him, which I thought might turn into 'seh-condary caregiver,' but...
jokes.setup: Ross trying to get Ben to say 'dada'
jokes.punchline: His desperate interpretation of a random sound as 'secondary caregiver'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-driven humor showing Ross's insecurity about his parenting role. Creative wordplay.

jokes.text: He's a baby, not a bomb.
jokes.setup: Chandler holding Ben very stiffly
jokes.punchline: Ross's observation about Chandler's extreme caution
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective comparison. Great economy and relatable observation.

jokes.text: This is how I would hold a football.
jokes.setup: Ross tells him to hold Ben like a football
jokes.punchline: Chandler's awkward demonstration shows he's equally bad with footballs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment showing his general physical incompetence. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Actually, I kind of think that we'll have two babies. You know, a boy and a girl. Hopefully, the girl will come first, so Ben won't feel competitive.
jokes.setup: Ross casually mentions thinking about their future
jokes.punchline: He's planned out their entire family structure after six weeks of dating
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy showing Ross's over-planning. The specificity makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: We won't wanna raise kids in the city, so we'll move to Scarsdale. We'll be far enough from our parents so we don't have to see them, but close enough that they can baby-sit.
jokes.setup: Ross continues his family planning
jokes.punchline: The detailed logistics including strategic parent placement
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with specific, relatable details about suburban family life. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: And yes, I know the taxes are a little higher than Nassau County, but the school system's great.
jokes.setup: Ross's detailed future planning
jokes.punchline: He's researched comparative tax rates and school systems
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation climax. The specific tax knowledge makes his planning both absurd and terrifyingly detailed.

jokes.text: I don't have a pot. Maybe I've got one at home. Or in Scarsdale. Hey, is that a door?
jokes.setup: Rachel panicking after Ross's future planning speech
jokes.punchline: Her desperate search for escape, including referencing his Scarsdale plan
jokes.explanation: Great panic response with good callback. The escalating desperation is well-constructed.

jokes.text: Murder, cancer, soccer teams eating each other in the Andes.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks what's happening as small talk
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's catalog of movie horrors with increasingly specific details
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation ending with specific Alive reference. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Charlotte who? With the web. The spider. She dies. She has babies and dies. It's like, 'Welcome home from the hospital.' Thud!
jokes.setup: Explaining Charlotte's Web plot
jokes.punchline: The brutal timing juxtaposition of birth and death with sound effect
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant observation about children's literature. The 'Thud!' sound effect is perfect punctuation.

jokes.text: I fell for that with Pride of the Yankees. I thought I'd see a film about Yankee pride. And boom! The guy gets Lou Gehrig's disease.
jokes.setup: Phoebe being fooled by happy-sounding titles
jokes.punchline: Her innocent expectation about a baseball movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic about movie titles. The innocence makes her disappointment funnier.

jokes.text: The guy was Lou Gehrig. Didn't you kind of see it coming?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's surprise about Lou Gehrig getting Lou Gehrig's disease
jokes.punchline: Rachel's logical observation about the obvious outcome
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Phoebe's innocence. Logical and well-delivered.

jokes.text: I just got my Pik-Up Stiks back from the shop.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions bringing 'nerves of steel' to game time
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Pick-Up Sticks requiring repair
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept delivered casually. The idea of repairing such a simple game is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: They don't start sentences with 'You know who just died shoveling snow?'
jokes.setup: Richard explaining why he likes hanging out with younger friends
jokes.punchline: Specific example of older people's morbid conversation starters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor about aging and mortality. Specific and relatable while being darkly funny.

jokes.text: Then, go Vassar! They're not in it. Okay, then just go.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to show sports enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Her complete lack of sports knowledge leading to giving up
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's complete sports ignorance. Character-appropriate defeat.

jokes.text: You'll just die or divorce or blow your pet's head off.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's nihilistic assessment based on her movie marathon
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the Old Yeller trauma. Dark escalation with great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: If not, I can learn how to do a bikini wax with leftover Christmas candles.
jokes.setup: Monica going to read Cosmo for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: The disturbing alternative of DIY beauty treatments with holiday supplies
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but funny image. The Christmas candle specificity adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: You've planned out the next 20 years. We've dated for six weeks!
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate future planning
jokes.punchline: The stark contrast between timeline and relationship length
jokes.explanation: Perfect mathematical observation highlighting the absurdity. Great contrast and economy.

jokes.text: The Big Book of Children's Names.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he just read about the name Emily somewhere
jokes.punchline: He admits to reading a specialized naming book
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment showing his thoroughness even in casual claims. Character-specific.

jokes.text: You're with a guy who won't stop planning his future with you. He knows we'll end up together. If that scares you, tough! You'll have to deal with that.
jokes.setup: Rachel's complaint about not wanting everything decided
jokes.punchline: Ross doubling down on his certainty with aggressive confidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross's stubbornness. Character-appropriate response to conflict.

jokes.text: I don't know if I was happier when George Bailey destroyed the business, or Donna Reed cried or when the pharmacist made his ear bleed.
jokes.setup: Phoebe supposedly enjoying It's a Wonderful Life
jokes.punchline: She only focused on the depressing parts of the supposedly uplifting movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations. Phoebe finds the darkness even in an uplifting classic.

jokes.text: It should've been called: 'It's a Sucky Life, And Just When You Think It Can't Suck Anymore, It Does!'
jokes.setup: Phoebe's negative experience with It's a Wonderful Life
jokes.punchline: Her alternative, pessimistic title
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay on the original title with perfect escalating structure. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Could that shot be any prettier?
jokes.setup: Watching someone make a foosball shot
jokes.punchline: Chandler's signature speech pattern applied to sports commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler catchphrase structure. Pure character comedy that became iconic.

jokes.text: Really? I didn't know they had foosball in the 1800s.
jokes.setup: Richard mentions having a foosball table in college
jokes.punchline: Joey's exaggerated age joke
jokes.explanation: Simple age joke but fits the pattern. Predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Nice mustache. At puberty, that thing will really kick in.
jokes.setup: Richard commenting on Joey's mustache
jokes.punchline: The implication that Joey hasn't hit puberty yet
jokes.explanation: Good comeback showing Richard can play their game. Well-constructed insult.

jokes.text: That's why we don't let her play.
jokes.setup: Monica easily scores at foosball
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual admission of excluding her
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective revelation about their sexism. Good economy.

jokes.text: He is so much cooler than our dads. Our dads are okay and all, but Richard is just—
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why they like Richard
jokes.punchline: The setup for the 'dad' revelation without realizing the implication
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the dad revelation. Creates anticipation for the uncomfortable moment.

jokes.text: You guys see me as a dad?
jokes.setup: The guys comparing him favorably to their fathers
jokes.punchline: Richard's hurt realization of how they categorize him
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with genuine emotion. The hurt in the question makes it both funny and sad.

jokes.text: When we say 'dad,' we mean 'buddy.' We mean 'pal.'
jokes.setup: Desperate attempt to backtrack from calling Richard a dad
jokes.punchline: Chandler's obvious lie about slang usage
jokes.explanation: Good desperation humor. The obvious lie makes the situation more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: No, seriously. Joey's my dad. Monica's my dad. I've even got some dads down at work.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to convince Richard that 'dad' means 'friend'
jokes.punchline: The absurd application to everyone, including Monica
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that becomes more absurd. 'Monica's my dad' is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Well, your other dad and I are gonna go have a romantic evening.
jokes.setup: Chandler's explanation about 'dad' meaning friend
jokes.punchline: Richard sarcastically using their logic while leaving hurt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comeback that turns their logic against them while expressing his hurt. Perfect delivery.

jokes.text: For a really cool guy, you suck at foosball.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to comfort Richard about feeling old
jokes.punchline: Her backhanded compliment approach
jokes.explanation: Typical Monica attempt at comfort that comes out wrong. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Calling it a 'poopy diaper' doesn't make this process any cuter.
jokes.setup: Someone using baby talk about diaper changing
jokes.punchline: Ross's realistic assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about parenting euphemisms. Relatable and well-delivered.

jokes.text: No, my Uncle Hi.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if Ben said the word 'hi'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic clarification about a fictional uncle
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay. Great economy and good sarcastic timing.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. I guess I just bring it out in him.
jokes.setup: Ross being upset about missing Ben's first word
jokes.punchline: Rachel's mock-serious claim of special baby-speaking abilities
jokes.explanation: Mild sarcasm that fits the situation. Not exceptional but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Suddenly I'm seeing him go off to college!
jokes.setup: Ben saying 'bye' for the first time
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately jumping to future milestones
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ross's tendency to project into the future. Character-consistent but predictable.

jokes.text: Now, I've looked ahead on the tape, and he does find him again. But before that happens, there's some rough going for a while.
jokes.setup: Phoebe preparing to watch Sesame Street with Ben
jokes.punchline: Her careful preview and warning about Ernie and Bert content
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback showing Phoebe's learned behavior. Treating Sesame Street like a potentially traumatic film is brilliantly absurd.

tmdb_overview: Monica's new boyfriend impresses Chandler and Joey. Phoebe regrets her missed childhood experiences. Rachel resents Ross planning their entire future.
